reviews.review_description: Before everthing DO NOT BUY THIS APP So I bought this app thinking it would be good until the stupid key allminment stage I tried everything to make this work iv turned on the light put bright lights iv used everything even the auto one didnt work it was allways of the keyboard or to many keys I have a regular piano btw one time it was even on the floor also you pay 10 dollars for this app and there is like no songs to play at all there is others but you have to pay a OTHER 10 dollar monthly subscription wich is terrible as it’s 10 dollars just to get the stupid game WHATEVER YOU DO DO NOT BUY THIS APP

developer_response.body:
There are 1000+ free songs that come with PianoVision that do not cost subscription at all. The subscription is for copyright material which is totally optional. One can upload any music to the app too that they legally obtain.

If you are having problems with alignment and can't troubleshoot it yourself you can always join the discord for assistance. Usually it is as simple as just holding your Occulus button down or setting your boundary/guardian correctly.

reviews.review_title: The bad reviews are ppl bad with tech
reviews.review_description: I have used the app thoroughly for the better part of a year now... YES the keyboard needs to be recalibrated occasionally but you should be able to have a solid 15 or 20 mins with no adjustments.. also be sure to use a cable with your headset to your keyboard it's just worth it for the reliability and ease of use. I wish I could buy the app outright instead of a subscription like a song book!

developer_response.body: Thank you, glad you're enjoying PianoVision!

reviews.review_title: What a brilliant App, I'm addicted!
reviews.review_description: I'm 3 days in and I don't want to put it down. I'm a Quest3 user with a standard upright acoustic piano. I've spent years trying to learn piano and I've managed to learn lots of bits of songs, but I can't sight read. I've just been really frustrated with my learning....until now  This has changed the game! Setup was easy although it required manual adjustments. The free songs didn't keep me sufficiently entertained but I'm not Australian so jingle bells with the blue skies and BBQs isn't really acceptable. So after the first few hours I subscribed and have been blasting bohemian rhapsody and bad habits. It has massively increased my confidence and I have loved playing songs through to completion. it's a really well devised app with a really professional UI. I hope more people discover this and give it a real go. Hopefully they keep developing this app and add some metronome features.

developer_response.body: We are very glad you enjoy PianoVision! Yes we are busy bees working to make it as good as it can be. If you haven't already, join our discord server to offer suggestion and be a part of the community!

reviews.review_title: great app for learning will keep using
reviews.review_description:
the bad got it a 1 star because of this, calibrating keyboard is a nightmare and you have to do it every time, the auto dos not help at all, also a built in sound option for playing would be nice, I have a normal midi keyboard for a daw, ether I have it connected to my headset or my pc. I have yet to play with treating my keyboard like a real piano using the app and playing sound out loud to test if it any good, so far this app dos not inspire confidence regarding the calibrating seeing everything is annoying or bad with calibrating, at least it knows I have a 61 key but auto calibrating thinks 25 key at all times. I might have payed for a subscription if the calibrating worked at all. and the ai is useless to me. lack of updates that do anything worth wile are not promising.

the good with some things I wish for, excellent key flow and fun to fallow the falling notes needs more games though, so far songs are all old public domain once that are kind of cute, works well enough.  wish it had more chord to learn like "pentatonic, natural and harmonic minor, whole tone, diminished, hexatonic, japanese, pelog, ying zhong, Lydian, Mixolydian, and Locrian", I wish. this is my wish list non of these are here but major and minor, what type of minor no clue. as a cheap piano app its amazing for what it is compared to more expensive options

developer_response.body:
Nice to hear you think the app is great and will continue to use it despite giving it a low rating. The vast majority of the scales/chords you ask for can be found in our Chord/Scale explorer.

You can always join the discord if you need technical assistance and help to find functions.

reviews.review_description: This is a great idea and I really wanted it to work! I have an acoustic upright piano and aside from setting the positions every time you log on like the other complaints, it just doesn’t really work with this style of piano. The app doesn’t recognise when you press the keys or hear the notes you are playing. You can sort of play along but it stops quite often unless you turn the song progress off. I had bought a few different songs and configured it pretty much perfectly but had 0% complete on all songs. The help guide and forum said it pretty much doesn’t work with this style of piano and to use a laptop instead, which is a waste of time when this was one of the reasons I bought a VR headset

developer_response.body: Meta Quest microphones do not process piano audio currently. If you download our desktop app we have an acoustic piano listening function so you can use the waiting notes in the app.

reviews.review_description: Not it it's hard to set up and to get any like actual songs you need to get a subscription. The subscription isn't too bad but there's no one-time subscription so you're just going to keep trying to pay for this and it's just going to be f****** annoying all the time
you need to take the time to adjust and calibrate the electronic keyboard over your real keyboard or piano. very fun after you do that work. could use more songs, hopefully they add more soon

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review, we appreciate it.

Currently there is over 1000 songs that come with PianoVision organised from grade 0 through to 12, there is also a holiday pack as well. Subscription gives access to some 10,000 copyright songs too. You can also upload songs you have to PianoVision too! 

If you'd like any help please feel free to join our discord if you haven't already, we have an amazing community there.

reviews.review_title: Love the app - just mini issue to 🚩
reviews.review_description:
The “recalibrate” and “welcome back” windows aren’t allowing me to click. I uninstalled and reinstalled the app and it allowed me to use it once but again when returning again (after meta quest turned off/ asleep) it again won’t allow me to click. Meta quest and app are both up to date.

*also didn’t love that I reactivated my subscription and it’s lost all my liked/ playlists. That’s a genuine shame.

I’ll change this review to 5 stars when it works again!

reviews.review_title: Good but a long way to go
reviews.review_description:
Not bad....but 
#1 no exit only way to get out is shut head set down
#2 have to reset keyboard every time I turn on.
#3 ui needs a good tidy up....more user friendly.
#4 if you going to have a chatbox helper make sure it actually works.....just repeats...can I help you.
Stll needs some work but has great prospects.
I'll review again in 6 months. Other wise I'm out.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, you can exit the app by pinching and activating the oculus button, that will take you out of the app and from there you can close it.

We are actively developing PV and hope to make it better and better. Please join our discord if you have the time, there is a nice community there, you can ask questions and post suggestions too! Thank you for your review.

reviews.review_description: Not good for business, I bought the app several years ago but stop playing because of $100.00 updates. You can’t even use for basic lessons.

developer_response.body:
There are no $100 updates. The one-time PianoVision purchase includes a large library of lessons, over 1,000 songs, the AR Sight Reader game to help develop reading skills, and many other features.

Over time, we've continued to add more content and functionality to the one-time purchase at no extra cost. The subscription is entirely optional and is only required for access to licensed copyright-protected content.

reviews.review_description: The calibration sucks and you only get to play old-school songs. hard to even learn songs when the keys are analigned

developer_response.body: You can upload songs to the headset. There are over 1000+ graded songs that come for free to help build your playing skills. If you'd like some technical support please join our discord we have a great community willing to help.

reviews.review_description: I purchased Plus for a month, but there are too many outdated sheet music pieces. There should be more modern music and more classical repertoire—there’s too much American style and not enough European. They should add the option to download additional music, even paid, so you can choose what you like to play. The calibration also needs improvement because it often loses alignment with the keys. If they improve these things, I would consider subscribing for a year. As it stands, it doesn’t work well—before asking people to spend money, they should make sure the app works properly.
It's too bad there's no free trial for the monthly sub before you get charged :(

developer_response.body: A free trial is not possible since copyright music cannot be given out for free. The good news is that you can upload songs to PianoVision, and there are over 1000 songs that come free with PianoVision organised from grade 0 to 12 to help build your skills.

reviews.review_description: Just absolutely amazing! This app needs to be on all VR formats and head sets!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind review, much appreciated. We are glad you are enjoying PianoVision, there is still a lot more to come!

reviews.review_description:
I am 43 yrs old and have been attempting to learn the piano for years. Rather, I have always wanted to, took lessons for 6 months and forced to take a break. I intended to pick it back up, but never did. 

Recently went out and bought a Roland Juno D8 that has MIDI and a full size weighted key bed. 

Piano vision isn't flawless but it definitely works. For the first time ever, I was able to sit and play by following along to a Ludovico Euinadi song called Nuvole Bienche. Never before have I been able to let myself go and feel the music like I was able to today. 

I am still not "good" but I have been playing. I am a monthly subscriber and have been doing the lessons. For now, its enough and I feel on the way to being an actual musician. Only time, effort and practice will get me there, but Pianovision made me feel like I was able to play before my skill level got me there. 

5 stars & would recommend. 

PS
1 stars are far too harsh. Its okay to be frustrated but those are not warranted at all.

reviews.review_title: Latest update made me quit subscription.
reviews.review_description:
I just played the latest Pianovision update, and I'm really frustrated with how much the frame rate has dropped. The keyboard position keeps shifting, and the calibration UI flow is completely broken. 

I also don’t like the new connected notes feature and turned it off right away. The lyrics feature is fun but when the position is lost between songs and the UI is z-fighting the whole thing feels broken.

Everyone makes mistakes when pushing updates, but maybe don't put out untested updates over the holidays.

Was planning to play piano with family over holidays, instead cancelled subscription.

You had a good app and broke it because you wanted to justify the subscription fees. This app was excellent 2 years ago. It delivered what was promised. I doubt the company knows the objective of the app now in its current state. It is an unusable mess with too many frictions. My advice is roll back  to what the app was when it launched and create a different app with your subscription and all the other bells and whistles. If the new app does not survive, then it's not meant to be 🙂.
I used to love this app but now it is sluggish and it deleted all my imported songs. I wish I could leave 0 stars.

reviews.review_title: Don't buy if you don't want to pay 10€/m
reviews.review_description: If you buy the game you get just a few christmas songs. To get all the songs you have to buy a 10€ monthly subscription

developer_response.body: There are over 1000 free songs that come with PianoVision. The songs in subscription are for copyright material which cannot legally be given for free.

reviews.review_description:
I am a pianist & have been a long-time user of PianoVision from its first release to the most recent update (which is a game changer by the way). I have stuck with this app, which has not been perfect, BUT I see its potential to be an unbelievable tool for anyone who ever wanted to play piano!  The developers are great & available on discord to help/listen. They have taken my suggestions/feedback over the years & implemented exactly what I thought would improve this app. For those who always wanted but never thought they would play the piano I challenge you to try this app & see how far you get in a short time. I LOVE the latest update which now includes a teaching mode that will walk a complete beginner through the basics of setup & how to start (hand placement, assignment) to playing scales & songs. I LOVE the subscription option which gives me access to many songs & more in depth training. I also LOVE the ability to import music; THAT has been a great feature for me especially when there is music I cannot find in the catalog or maybe music that I have created myself & would like to quickly play. 
Are there quirks with this app? Yes. Does this app work perfectly? NO but what does? Are the developers consistently working on addressing those quirks? YES.
What I look forward to seeing is the update to the teaching tool that introduces reading sheet music & translating that visually to the piano. I have made several suggestions to the development team & have faith that if it's possible, they will implement updates. I think giving this app 1,2 stars is unfair & outrageous! I am guessing those low stars come from people who 1. have not given this app a real go 2. do not understand the complexity of creating something that is not a game but a tool to help anyone play the piano. I see a great future with this app.
I cannot wait to see how far this app will go. I encourage anyone to give this a real chance. Remember: Perfection is the enemy of Progress daniebanes

reviews.review_title: Don't Let the Negative Reviews Fool You
reviews.review_description:
I honestly do not understand the wave of negative reviews surrounding this update. I have commented before on the subscription model, and I will repeat it here: yes, there is a monthly fee on top of the base price, but this is clearly communicated. Licensing modern music is not free, and the developer has to cover those costs somehow. Expecting continuously updated, licensed content without an ongoing fee is simply unrealistic.

As for the app itself, it is excellent. I have used it extensively, and if you have the dedication to actually learn piano, this app gives you everything you need to make real progress. It works for all skill levels, from beginners to advanced players, and offers genuinely unique and extremely useful features that set it apart from anything else available.

Personally, I have learned several classical pieces using this app, despite not being fluent at reading sheet music. Although I attended music school for a year, my playing ability has always been stronger than my reading skills. This app supports a more intuitive, hands-on way of learning that plays to those strengths.
The MIDI functionality in particular has been a complete game changer for me. Being able to play along, receive real-time feedback, and even upload your own MIDI files (which opens the door to learning virtually any piece) makes this app incredibly powerful.
Regarding the new update: yes, there are still some bugs. But instead of leaving purely negative reviews, it would be far more productive to provide constructive feedback and give the developers the chance to improve it. In my experience, the new features and updated UI are welcome additions to an already outstanding app.

In short, if you want to learn piano and are willing to put in some effort, this app is absolutely worth the investment.
App does not respond to hand tracking.

developer_response.body: The app does respond to hand tracking, if you need technical assistance please join our Discord.

reviews.review_description:
I just got this app a few days ago, I was hesitant at first because all the negative reviews until I read some stuff on discord. I have a Roland FP-30X and it instantly connects up and knows it’s an 88 key, keyboard. It’s easy to navigate and choose what you want, there’s like 1000 songs on here. Load it up, it’s super smooth and overlays the song in the background of your keyboard and makes learning super easy. The review bombers couldn’t be more wrong, if you want to learn piano, you’ll love this.   

Can you get more if you pay? Yes!  Will I pay? Probably once I get through the lessons and 1000 songs. 

My ONLY gripe is the hand detection is a bit hit or miss at times for selecting songs and stuff.

The app was ok before, so so tracking, ok music selection, somewhat intuitive UI.
But this new update is terrible, they removed Claire de Lune Debussy that I loved to play, but mainly the performance is terrible compared to before (feels like 30 FPS) and makes me sick now. If they don’t fix that, I’m cancelling my subscription. 
The new calibration is somehow even worse than the previous version and my screens often disappear from my view, forcing me to restart the app more often than before.
Also, as all VR devs like to do, they redesigned everything in their UI, and it’s worse than before too! 
Feels less complete, less options in one screen, and still the same terrible search function (but now with artists lookup that is very welcome)
Completely broken, can’t calibrate at all. Shows piano on the floor. Tried uninstall & reinstalling. Completely useless app

developer_response.body: Join the discord if you are having technical issues. It is almost always an easy fix that users have missed.

reviews.review_description: After reading the reviews i still thought it couldnt be that bad but the tracking is horrible it took so long to be able to select a song and the songs arent even correct theyre all transposed to an easier version

developer_response.body:
There are over 1000+ songs in PianoVision given for free many are full versions at the very lower levels they are simplified by design so users don't get overwhelmed by the difficulty, that helps you get better. 

If you need technical assistance please join the Discord, we have a lot of helpful resource there.

reviews.review_description:
This app is great! As others have mentioned there seems to be a lot of negative reviews after the last update, not entirely sure why. The app is a little buggy and pretty much does need the subscription to be good, but it’s still a new concept and people don’t work for free, so it’s understandable.

I love the app personally. I have the subscription and it gives you access to tons of popular songs. I get to play my favorite songs on piano without actually knowing how to play piano. It’s like the piano music tutorial on YouTube where the notes fall down onto the keys, but in real life. Also there are fun games and tutorials to help you actually learn piano.

Ok so some of the things that need fixing. The auto-setup where it tries to identify where you keys are sucks. But it isn’t that hard to setup manually so no big deal. After using the app for like an hour something always seems to break and sometimes restarting doesn’t fix it, though when I come back to it the next day it always works again. 

And now some suggestions. There is a cool feature where it will make you repeat little bits of songs to help you memorize them. Unfortunately this is only for a select number of songs, please add the ability to do initiate this for any song or portion of music. Also some kind of feature to download 3rd party songs or make your own.

“Enhanced” Keyboard placement does not even come close to working. Manual placement is even harder. An AI voice will ambush you while the entire thing is malfunctioning. How has it gotten worse with each subsequent update up to the point of completely breaking it??

reviews.review_title: The reason why I bought oculus quest
reviews.review_description:
I had problem to connect it to my midi piano, but now it works again.
Goood

I did not expect the Virtual Keyboard to be perfect, but I expect it to be usable. They are asking money for it after all. It certainly must be usable, right? It is not. At all. 

The hand tracking is so terrible, it is difficult to hit even the menu buttons. When you try to use more than one finger, it is completely lost. Playing any chord is impossible. It cannot register all my fingers. Instead it register several phantom fingers all around the keyboard. On a very simple, very slow song I got accuracy of: 0 out of 77 notes.

This needs a lot of work. Do work on it. I'd love to use it.

It's not worth the 10 dollars, you need to buy a whole pack just to play actual games songs, because who wants to learn boring songs, I was trying to play Star wars I have to buy it like what. And the automatic thingy where you select your piano is jacked

reviews.review_title: Best piano app ever if not for issues
reviews.review_description:
In concept this app is amazing but the bugs and latest updates just kill any praise i can give it. 
The new update that made it unplayable is the scrolling speed of the notes now. it's super choppy and feels like half the current framerate. It is insanely disorienting and annoying making it unplayable for me. (THIS IS SO BAD THAT IM LEAVING THIS REVIEW AT ONE STAR UNTIL PATCHED)
Other longstanding issues i have are:
Use of AI: like look at the thumbnail on the store for the christmas update. Really? and pushing that stupid voice assistant is annoying
Locking more public domain through subscription: I dont understand why theres classical stuff only available through subscription. I feel like I have played all the songs longer than 1 minute to death already in the base catalogue.
Calibration: the new auto calibration is atrocious for me.
Importing songs: the app is annoying to go one by one importing songs. I want to drag and drop and not have there be a limit to MIDIs
basic is trash, calibration too, and everything is locked behind a subscription

developer_response.body: Basic provides a huge amount of features including 1000+ free songs, games, lessons, uploads etc. Subscription is mostly for the copyright material which cannot legally be given for free.

reviews.review_description: What a piece of garbage. I can't even calibrate the keyboard properly regardless of the manual or the beta feature. It keeps locking quest 3 so you can't quit. Black screen all over. Unsubscribing.

When i got this app, there was no subscription. It had nearly 4.5 stars and included everything. I wasn't to upset with the change to the subcription model, but the new update makes using the basic mode so difficult i cant even use the app effectively. I fell like i got scammed. They shouldn't be able to do this.

Used to be a great app.   Then they took away being able to have the sheet music with the falling notes which costs them nothing extra btw.   The tracking is horrible now the keys always shift while playing.  They also took the highlighted key to play away and replaced with constantly highlighted C keys which is really annoying to see.

developer_response.body: Feel free to join the discord, we have a great community and we are always willing to help out. A lot of these issues you are facing can easily be troubleshooted.

reviews.review_description:
If you are thinking to buy this to learn piano - just dont.
App is jerky and laggy. Interface just doesn't work right, and always finds new way to get you out of tranquility.
Subscription is awful too: has allmost 0 interesting and modern songs to play. Import featire is good but fpr some reason requires PC and also has awful and jerky interface.
Truily bad expirience.

reviews.review_title: Not good enough for a subscription
reviews.review_description: I highly do not recommend getting this app. I paid for what I thought was a reasonable amount for a "just okay" piano app and now they want a subscription. I really feel robbed. Also the piano setup has got a lot worse. In my opinion avoid.
I'm using an Arturia KeyLab Essential 49 MK III to connect, but no sound is playing. Other people seem to be able to connect and use it immediately. I'm not sure, but I'm very frustrated.

Unfortunately every update gets worse because the dev is very inexperienced 
 
V1 was just about usable but its got worse every update as dev just adds more broken features without fixing old ones

reviews.review_title: I just bought it...what a mistake
reviews.review_description: I ignored the reviews and boy o boy what a mistake... You cant even close the app, forgets previous settings, borderline unusable
I thought I could just learn to play songs easier but everything is just behind a pay wall.

developer_response.body: There are 1000+ songs that are given for free, the completely optional subscription is mostly for copyright music which cannot be given for free.

reviews.review_description:
As everybody comments the new version is horrible…keyboard move continuously can’t find my basics …they tried to force me to buy subscription 
No good for the creators 
I know is a business but there’s more options
Will say goodbye if they don’t change as many other subscribers

Putting out trailers for a new update with a lot of new features and then revealing they are all behind a subscription service was not consumer friendly. I paid full price for the full app quite a while back and now it is a ‘basic’ version, with pop ups and parts of the UI which are locked. Bad business practice.

developer_response.body: Hey! We try to make the 2 tiers very clear in the app description. Nothing from the basic version is paywalled behind the subscription.

reviews.review_description: I tried to play it and the game play was horrible and I tried to return but I can’t

reviews.review_title: One star is one too many
reviews.review_description: Very bad experience from my first try. The keyboard goes to the floor and is opposite from me. Terrible!  Uninstalling and requesting a refund.
One star from me also because i bought the app in the first place and everything was fine but after a while i decided to play again and the app was asking for subscription.

Tried it out on what I assume to be V2 for the first time and loved it. It updated over night and my favorite songs were deleted, the manual calibration is buggy now and takes a lot more manual adjustment and still isn't exactly right. The auto calibration doesn't work at all. The UI is terrible and clunky at best. You should be able to grab the keyboard and resize it that way for coarse adjustments and use the adjuster for fine adjustments. Really disappointed with it to be honest. Going to give it some time but it's angered me enough to write this review. Also, "over 10000 songs" is misleading, as 90% of them aren't piano pieces and the ones that are aren't accurate to the actually sheet music.
Started the initial lessons. The learning is well though out, the piano calibration was fast an easy. I did the manual option.

developer_response.body: I'm glad you enjoy the lessons! There are a lot of them there and its great to take your time to go through them. Appreciate your support.

reviews.review_title: Paid for it year ago and now..
reviews.review_description: It's subscription based now? Thought I paid for it already.... Great

I really like the app and how fun it is to play piano. After the lastest update however the frame rate has dropped a bunch. If you try to use the AR feature such as adding a black background to on coming notes it completely breaks the key positioning.  And if anyone knows the answer to this, why does my keyboard sometimes play the notes im supposed to play? It'll do it randomly for some songs.

I was excited to try this app, but the experience was disappointing. The base version requires an active internet connection just to play songs, with no option to download them directly to the headset for offline play. Importing MIDI files also requires a separate external computer app, which adds unnecessary friction. This is especially frustrating because the App Store listing states that no internet connection is required. That description is misleading and should be clarified.

reviews.review_title: Great app for beginners to experts
reviews.review_description: This app has game-i-fied learning the piano, if you a beginner, everything is set out to make it enjoyable and easy to get into,  if your intermediate, there is a learning engine inbuilt to push you and you can reduce the visual helpers, if your an expert ( 20 fingers type lol ), falling notes is probably the fastest way to build up muscle memory for new complex pieces.   I have used this app since applab,  i was totally none piano user before, now i can learn and play pieces i enjoy on an evening.

I really liked this app when I first got it but it’s recently gotten too glitchy. The menu is unresponsive, adjusting the view and keyboard has become too much of a pain. I hope this program gets better.
impossible to get the virtual piano to set, it always ends up under the floor. it seems they have messed it up.

reviews.review_title: A MR app that is genuinely useful
reviews.review_description:
PianoVision is one of the rare VR apps that goes beyond novelty — it’s genuinely useful. It’s been exactly what I needed to finally start to learn to read music instead of just copying YouTube tutorials.

I’ve learned plenty of harder songs in the past by watching videos and memorizing hand positions, but I never properly learned the staff. The Cow UFO game is honestly one of the most useful things I’ve found for drilling note recognition — it makes memorizing the notes on the staff feel natural instead of painful.

I’m using this with a Roland Juno DS-88 via MIDI and it was totally plug-and-play. No hassle, it just worked immediately.

Also: the basic version is absolutely worth the asking price, especially for a beginner. There are lots of included/free songs, and they’re more than enough to keep me challenged for a long time without needing to buy anything extra.   The included holiday songs are also a nice bonus.

Calibration: the automatic calibration didn’t work for me, but the manual calibration works great and gets everything lined up well. My only minor frustration is that calibration can reset sometimes when you take the headset off.

Big shoutout to Zac (the developer) — I took him up on the offer to try previous versions and he walked me through it on Discord. Super helpful and responsive.

If you have a MIDI keyboard and want something that actually makes you practice and improve, this is a no-brainer.
I bought this app years ago and loved playing Fur Elise, but now it’s locked behind an expensive subscription service… hate this app now

reviews.review_title: My favorite on the meta quest
reviews.review_description: First time ever I feel like I’m learning and it’s a lot of fun.  My Bluetooth didn’t work so I had to use the printer cable. The only down side is I have no way of charging the meta quest to keep playing lol

I was charged ten bucks just to download the app and view the tutorial, to play the game you’re required to buy a subscription, at ten dollars a month. Don’t download! Unless you’re willing to pay monthly .

developer_response.body: You are not required to buy a subscription to use the app, it is 100% optional. There are a lot of features in the base app and we are actively developing and adding more content there. Do take time to see what you can do, I'm sure you will be pleased. Join the discord if you'd like more help we have a great community there.

reviews.review_description: So buggy , and its not a finished product. Not worth it. The fact that they want a subscription for this is crazy.

reviews.review_title: Slow fps, and twitchy finger tracking
reviews.review_description: Hand tracking isnt up to my expectations, it just inputs whatever it thinks at times especially under good lighting and a propor placement for my keyboard, not really a good teaching thing until they fix this. Otherwise I love it and its so creative

reviews.review_title: Don't buy until they fix it
reviews.review_description: Bad UI , lack of new songs with premium, super wordy lessons written by AI

I love this game it makes learning and playing the Piano 10x simpler and fun i dont recomend the Non real piano i recommed the midi or left Option for playing the App its just better i Like the free Songs that you get from the App for me im german its just sooo cool that it give O chrismas tree and many other Songs maybe one that YOU Even know and dont listen to the 1,2,3 Stars Reviews they just mad bcs they can’t Play the Piano

This is the first app I've written a review for, but I'm writing it to level out the bad reviews because it's amazing. For someone who really struggles with reading sheet music, this has unlocked many songs I've always wanted to play. Moreover, it's helping me read sheet music because I can learn to play the song, then switch to the sheet music and pair the notes/hand movements to it. I only use the basic version, and what I can do with that feels limitless because you can import your own midi data into it. I think I'm having such a good experience because I'm using a midi keyboard that's plugged in. I will admit that using a virtual keyboard was not a very good experience - so I wouldn't try this if you don't actually have a keyboard or piano. However, from what I can tell, a lot of what people are complaining about is that there's a subscription to gain rights for songs instead of the hand tracking. Just note that the developer has said that the subscriptions were added when the paid songs were added (you can verify this by watching old reviews with the base app), and everything in the base app remained free. If people are worried about not having access to heaps of songs without paying the subscription, all the need to do is find midi data of the songs they want to play and upload them into the basic version.

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks for the review. We really appreciate you bringing the reality of the situation to light. We try very hard to make it clear that you don't need the subscription, and we worked really hard to make available the songs people love, for those who are okay with a subscription. And to keep the $10 version a strong offering for those who aren't.

reviews.review_description: Why make me pay for the game just to pay even more for a subscription not even a good song library and dosent accommodate my 61 key keyboard
The best use of passthrough mode I have seen in a VR app. I was bashing out tunes on the piano in minutes that would have taken days to learn.
Nice idea, bad experience.

As a long-time Meta headset user, I can say Piano VR is unmatched on Quest. The latest update is a big step forward, and the Discord support team is amazing. Shout-out to Zak for guiding me through the Beta install. Highly recommended!
The tracking is so terrible definitely don’t recommend SAVE YOUR MONEY!

I purchased this for my son who is disabled. He doesn’t have a piano that he can plug in. He cannot read sheet music. He just wanted to try and play along with songs that he knows. After purchasing the game, we found out that the tunes that he recognises are not available with the free version. He keeps complaining that the subscription page keeps popping up and he is finding it difficult to clear it off the screen. Before we got over the 2 hour trial period, I emailed the developer asking what he meant in his comments about version 1 and version 2. After 6 days, I still haven’t received a reply. The cost of the subscription is far too expensive for us.

UPDATE …Here is a copy of the email sent to you on 30/09/2025

KHi. I purchased this app for my son yesterday. Only then did we realise that the tunes available are rather limited. I am not in a position to be paying a monthly subscription for The full catalogue of songs. I have told my son to stop using the game until I clarify this situation.Please explain what you mean when you say that the customer can have version 1 or version 2.
Kind regards, Peter Collins.’

developer_response.body: Hey! I didn't see an email from you. Feel free to reach out to zac@pianovision.app and I'll definitely respond.

reviews.review_description: This is such a waste of money. Please do NOT buy

reviews.review_title: Please make a per song/pack fee!!!
reviews.review_description: I love this app, but the subscription sucks! Please do like BeatSaber! Offer a per-song fee or a music pack option. It would save this app from oblivion.

developer_response.body: The subscription model allows you access to some 10,000 copyright songs, which is quite enormous compared to most other piano apps, its the most cost effective way to access it. You can also upload your own songs to the app by using the free desktop app found on our website. PianoVision is being actively worked on so do check in for updates, hopefully you can find something that you enjoy.

reviews.review_description: Even when you get the keyboard calibration to work by the time you look at the accept button and click it the calibration has moved.

reviews.review_title: Buy just a very basic helper
reviews.review_description: Monthy payment is a rip off. Should be free and have subs if you want them. I feel double charged

developer_response.body: Subscription is completely optional and there are plenty of features to be discovered in the base app. We are actively developing PianoVision and are adding lots of new features. Do check in again and I hope you can find some enjoyment!

reviews.review_description:
Tl:dr; great app if you have a MIDI keyboard 

let’s you Connect to a midi piano, upload sheet music pdfs and midi from your computer so you can learn and play any song you want as long as you don’t have room temperature IQ

 I haven’t tried the virtual or acoustic piano but I imagine they aren’t as responsive or fluid to play, I’m sure it’s serviceable enough if you’re dipping your toes in learning but I heavily recommend using a midi keyboard. I have a cheap Casio CT 700 and it works great. If all you have is a quest and nothing else, you’ll have a difficult time getting full use of this app. 

The amount of free features the app provides is generous and the paid features offer a lot of convenience but not necessary to enjoy the app. 
I’ve been following the app since it first released and I’m impressed to see how far they’ve come and all the features that have been added. While the changes between version and monetization could have been handled better, I still appreciate all the hard work put in to provide useful tool that can make learning piano fun and convenient.

Pianovision has been a real game changer for me, I have tried to learn the piano for years but have never stayed committed! However since playing Pianovision, I have been truly engrossed and engaged, I am really enjoying learning more and more every day and it’s an app I highly recommend to anyone that is considering it, you will not regret it!
The app cant work at all after the update

reviews.review_title: Why do you not want money?
reviews.review_description:
Imagine this app without a subscription and a reasonable price with an occasional sale. More people buy the app, consistent updates, everyone’s happy and rating goes up. App gains popularity, more people buy, app becomes a hit and generates lots of revenue. 

But no lets do subscription because that’s clearly bringing in more customers 🙃

developer_response.body: Hey, we want to offer people a large library of music they want to learn and play. To do that, we need to license music, which requires recurring royalty payments, which only works with a subscription.

reviews.review_description: They make you pay for a buggy as hell app and then they put a subscription wall before you can use it.. I hate this

I would like to see more songs like the rest of Beethoven’s moonlight sonata movements 2,3 and 4. Please and thank you. I’ve been practicing nearly every day and it’s fun making a lot of noticeable progress. but I’m getting ready for the rest of the peace.
A little bit of an update after being directed that I can add my own midi files. This is such a dynamic app. I use it nearly every day and love melting away, making beautiful noise. ￼
